signing_authority: Jon Amos, Head of Product & Operations

system_description.overview: Craniometrix is a healthcare technology company that provides an all-in-one dementia platform. The platform delivers software and services to healthcare organizations to support patients living with dementia and their families, with specific alignment to the Medicare GUIDE program.

products.Intake and Alignment: Supports healthcare organizations to intake patients and families; provides alignment data for the Medicare GUIDE program
products.Need Assessment and Care Planning: Assesses needs of patients living with dementia and their families; delivers tailored care plans; supports referrals to additional services including respite care
products.Billing and Compliance Management: Provides data necessary for accurate billing and compliance with Medicare program requirements

infrastructure.physical_presence: No physical offices or servers; operates from a co-working space; all data processed on cloud services

network_architecture.network_segmentation: Segmented VPCs with access control lists (ACLs); network segmentation implemented to separate systems and data based on sensitivity and criticality
network_architecture.api_gateway: Application gateway with system firewalls configured
network_architecture.firewalls: System firewalls configured on the application gateway and production network to limit unnecessary ports, protocols and services; firewall rules reviewed annually by IT management
network_architecture.ids_ips: IDS and IPS via AWS to identify and protect against threats in conjunction with security policy network settings
network_architecture.vpn: Remote access via bastion host with VPN
network_architecture.tls: TLS using certificates from established third-party certificate authority for all web-based user requests; TLS-enabled on Supabase Postgres connections
network_architecture.content_filtering: Content filtering system via proxy server; all systems restricted to internet through proxy
network_architecture.topology: Public Internet -> Netlify CDN (static React app on S3) -> AWS North Virginia backend app VM (HTTPS/TLS, SSH) -> Supabase North Virginia (managed Postgres with TLS). Identity via Supabase Auth and Rippling Workforce SSO/MFA. Secrets managed through Doppler. Outbound integrations to OpenAI API, Social AI API, Calaim API. Observability via Sentry, Vanta, 7th Logs, and Grafana. GitHub for source control and dependencies.

application_architecture.environments: Separate development/test and production environments; changes tested in separate environment before production deployment (CC8.1.4)

authentication_access_control.identity_provider: Supabase Auth (end users), Rippling Workforce SSO/MFA (employees)
authentication_access_control.mfa: MFA enforced for administrative access to production platform (CC6.1.1); MFA enforced for all remote access; MFA required for source code tool access; two-factor authentication required for email access
authentication_access_control.password_policy: Controlled through AWS Password policy with periodic forced changes, password expiry and complexity requirements; accounts disabled after limited unsuccessful logon attempts; no guest/anonymous logins permitted

encryption.key_management: Cloud provider KMS; access to encryption keys restricted to authorized personnel only (CC6.1.9)

ci_cd_devops.ci_cd_tool: Not explicitly named; internal tool tracks changes from initiation through deployment and validation
ci_cd_devops.branch_protection: Repository branch rules configured to ensure every merge request to move changes to production requires additional approval (CC8.1.2); approval process followed prior to merging to master branch
ci_cd_devops.iac: Infrastructure as code with baseline configurations retained; configuration management tool used for implementation and rollback capability (CC7.1.1)
ci_cd_devops.patch_management: Automatic patch management with monthly reviews by CTO/Management; workstations configured for automatic updates and scanned for patch compliance daily

monitoring_logging.alerting: Automated alerts configured for predefined thresholds; incident management process invoked for confirmed events and anomalies

third_party_services.purpose: Cloud infrastructure and data center services (subservice organization)
Managed Postgres database, authentication, and backups (North Virginia)
CDN, static site hosting for React frontend
Workforce management, SSO, and MFA for employees
Application performance monitoring and error tracking
Source code management and version control

security_tools.antivirus: Anti-virus and malware protection installed on all production servers (continuous scanning) and Mac-based workstations/laptops; definitions updated weekly for production servers, daily for workstations
security_tools.vulnerability_scanning: Monthly automated vulnerability scans on infrastructure and applications using automated tool; frequency adjusted as required (CC7.1.2)
security_tools.penetration_testing: Annual external penetration testing by independent third-party; results reviewed by IT management (CC4.1.1)

untestable: CC2.2.3 - Significant changes to people, roles and responsibilities could not be tested because there were no significant changes during the engagement period
CC3.2.1 - Cybersecurity insurance effectiveness could not be tested because there were no cyber incidents reported during the engagement period
CC6.5.2 - Customer data deletion upon termination could not be tested because there were no customer terminations during the engagement period
CC7.3.1 - Incident response effectiveness could not be tested because there were no security incidents reported during the engagement period
excluded_criteria: CC6.4 - Physical access controls excluded because Craniometrix has no physical servers; operates entirely in cloud (AWS responsible as subservice org)
A1.2 - Environmental protections excluded because Craniometrix operates from a co-working space; environmental controls managed by building management / AWS
Processing Integrity - Determined not relevant to the system
Privacy - Determined not relevant to the system
notable_controls: CC8.1.2 - Branch protection with merge request approval required for production deployments
CC8.1.3 - Source code changes logged, time-stamped, attributed to author; access to source code tool restricted with MFA
CC6.1.1 - MFA enforced for all user accounts with administrative access to production
CC7.4.1 - BCDR policy documented and tested annually with defined loss scenarios
CC7.4.2 - Daily backups with multi-AZ configuration and alerting for backup failures
CC6.1.7 - Network segmentation based on sensitivity and criticality
CC2.1.2 - Risk and Governance Executive Committee (RGEC) with semiannual reviews
CC3.1.1 - IT Leadership Committee meets weekly to review security activities and processes
CC9.2.1 - Vendor management with annual SOC report reviews and quarterly assessments for critical vendors without SOC reports

infrastructure.rationale: Single region (us-east-1) with multi-AZ is adequate but not resilient. Heavy reliance on Supabase as a managed platform rather than direct AWS services. No multi-region failover. Netlify CDN provides good edge delivery for frontend. No serverless or container orchestration mentioned.

application_architecture.rationale: React frontend with a backend VM is a reasonable architecture but not microservices. Limited technology detail disclosed - no specific languages, frameworks, or API patterns confirmed beyond React. Separate environments and branch protection are positives. No mention of automated testing pipelines.

data_layer.rationale: Single database type (Supabase Postgres) with managed backups and multi-AZ. Daily backups confirmed and tested. Data classification and retention policies in place. However, no caching layer, no data warehouse, and a single DB type creates concentration risk. Per-tenant segregation confirmed (CC6.1.3).

security_posture.rationale: Strong fundamentals: MFA enforced, IDS/IPS, firewalls, endpoint protection with antivirus, monthly vulnerability scanning, annual pen testing, cybersecurity insurance, Vanta for compliance automation. Network segmentation with ACLs. Log protection enabled. No WAF mentioned, and no named SIEM tool beyond Vanta. Secrets management via Doppler is a strong practice.

devops_maturity.rationale: GitHub for source control with MFA-protected access and branch protection rules. IaC with configuration management and rollback capability. Separate environments for dev/test and production. Emergency change process documented. However, CI/CD tool not explicitly named, no container orchestration, and automated testing pipeline not described.

bcdr_readiness.rationale: BCDR policy documented and reviewed annually. Annual testing of DR plans with defined loss scenarios. Daily backups with multi-AZ and backup failure alerting. Multiple servers across availability zones for failover. However, no multi-region failover capability, and RTO/RPO are not disclosed. DR plan relies on single region.

vendor_diversity.rationale: Good vendor diversity with 10+ distinct vendors across infrastructure (AWS, Supabase, Netlify), identity (Rippling, Supabase Auth), observability (Sentry, Grafana, Vanta), secrets (Doppler), AI (OpenAI), and source control (GitHub). Formal vendor management program with annual SOC reviews and quarterly assessments. Some concentration risk on Supabase for both database and auth.

overall.rationale: Craniometrix demonstrates solid security fundamentals for an early-stage healthcare company with a clean audit (zero exceptions on tested controls). The network diagram reveals a modern cloud-native stack with appropriate separation of concerns. Key strengths are MFA enforcement, branch protection, vendor management, and BCDR testing. Primary concerns are single-region deployment, limited technology detail disclosure in the report, reliance on managed platforms without clear fallback strategies, and absence of WAF. The 3-month audit period is notably short.

red_flags: Very short audit period of only 3 months (April 25 - July 25, 2025), which is the minimum for a Type 2 and suggests this is their first SOC 2
Single region deployment (us-east-1 only) with no multi-region failover for a healthcare platform processing sensitive patient data
No WAF (Web Application Firewall) mentioned for a public-facing healthcare application
RTO and RPO not disclosed despite handling critical healthcare data
Processing Integrity and Privacy trust criteria excluded from scope despite being a healthcare data platform
yellow_flags: 4 controls could not be tested due to no events occurring during the short engagement period (no incidents, no terminations, no personnel changes)
CI/CD tooling not explicitly named - may indicate manual or semi-automated deployment processes
No caching layer or data warehouse mentioned, suggesting early-stage data architecture
Concentration risk on Supabase for both primary database and authentication
TLS version not specified (could be using older TLS versions)
No mention of automated testing pipeline or test coverage metrics
Backend appears to run on a single VM rather than auto-scaling or containerized compute
green_flags: Zero exceptions noted across all 62 tested controls
Strong secrets management via Doppler (not hardcoded or in environment variables)
MFA enforced across production access, source code, email, and remote access
Branch protection with merge request approval required for production
Comprehensive vendor management program with annual SOC reviews and quarterly assessments
IT Leadership Committee meets weekly with Risk and Governance Executive Committee providing oversight
Daily backups with multi-AZ configuration and backup failure alerting
Annual penetration testing by independent third-party
Data classification policy established and documented
Network segmentation with ACLs and IDS/IPS
key_observations: Network diagram reveals a modern JAMstack-like architecture: static React frontend on Netlify CDN -> AWS backend VM -> Supabase managed Postgres, which is unusual for a healthcare platform (most use traditional server-rendered architectures)
Outbound AI integrations (OpenAI, Social AI, Calaim APIs) suggest the platform uses AI/ML for dementia care assessment, which is a differentiator but also a data privacy consideration for PHI
The company uses Rippling for workforce SSO/MFA but Supabase Auth for end-user authentication, indicating a deliberate separation of employee and customer identity planes
Signing authority is Head of Product & Operations (not CTO or CISO), suggesting a lean startup team without dedicated security leadership
The org chart shows only 4 direct reports to CEO (Head of Product & Operations, Head of Engineering, Head of Growth, Head of Medical Affairs) indicating a very small company
Despite being cloud-native with no physical offices, the report includes generic boilerplate about Windows/Network teams and proxy servers, suggesting the audit firm used template language
Medicare GUIDE program alignment is a specific federal program focus, indicating government healthcare as the primary market with associated regulatory requirements beyond SOC 2
